title: Nexus Conformance Test Fixture
description: Declarative test case for Nexus MCP tool behavioral verification. Each fixture must declare exactly one of: action (single tool invocation), event (single lifecycle event), or steps (multi-step sequence).

test_id.description: Unique identifier for this fixture, used for reporting

description.description: Human-readable description of what behavior this fixture verifies

covers.description: Declaration of which schema fields and return value paths this fixture verifies. At least one of state_schemas or return_value must be non-empty.

state_schemas.description: Map of state-schema filename (e.g. 'plan.schema.json') to array of field paths verified by this fixture's postcondition. Field paths use dot-notation with array index notation (e.g. 'issues[].status').

items.description: Field path within the named schema (e.g. 'id', 'issues[].status', 'issues[].how_agents')

return_value.description: Map of abstract tool name (e.g. 'plan_start') to array of return-value field paths verified by this fixture's postcondition.

items.description: Field path within the tool's return value (e.g. 'created', 'plan_id', 'issueCount')

description.description: Optional human-readable explanation of coverage rationale or known gaps

uncovered_params.description: Params intentionally excluded from postcondition verification. Validators treat entries here as explicit exceptions rather than coverage gaps, preventing false anti-pattern warnings.

items.description: Parameter name from the action or event params that is deliberately not verified

precondition.description: State that must be established before the action or steps are executed

state_files.description: Map of file path to content object (null means the file must not exist)

action.description: Single tool invocation — use for single-action fixtures; mutually exclusive with event and steps

event.description: Single lifecycle event — use for event-based fixtures that require no tool call; mutually exclusive with action and steps

steps.description: Ordered sequence of tool invocations or lifecycle events with per-step assertions — use for multi-step scenarios; mutually exclusive with action and event

postcondition.description: Assertions evaluated after the action (or all steps) complete

tool.description: Abstract tool name (e.g. plan_start, task_add). Must not include harness-specific prefixes.

params.description: Input parameters passed verbatim to the tool

event.description: A lifecycle event that triggers harness-level behavior without a direct tool call. Used for verifying runtime.json and agent-tracker.json state changes.

type.description: Lifecycle event type. session_start/session_end: harness session lifecycle; agent_spawn/agent_complete/agent_resume: agent instance lifecycle.

params.description: Optional event-specific parameters (e.g. agent_type and agent_id for agent_spawn)

description.description: Optional human-readable explanation of the event and its expected effect

postcondition.description: Assertions on the state and return value after the action completes

state_files.description: Map of file path to assertion object (null means the file must not exist after the action)

oneOf.description: JSONPath assertions on the file content. Keys are JSONPath expressions, values are expected values or matcher objects.
Matcher object for type checks and range assertions

pattern.description: Regex pattern the value must match (for type: string)

return_value.description: JSONPath assertions on the tool return value. Same assertion syntax as state_files entries.

error.description: If true, the action must produce an error response

error_contains.description: The error message must contain this substring (implies error: true)

step.description: One step in a multi-step scenario. Each step must contain exactly one of: action (tool invocation) or event (lifecycle event).

description.description: Optional human-readable label for this step

assert_return.description: JSONPath assertions on the return value of this step's action

assert_state.description: Map of file path to assertions on state files after this step completes. null means file must not exist.
